# Copilot Instructions

## Draft files

- Active drafts are `draft-*.md` files in the repository root. Do not look
  for drafts in subdirectories; the old per-directory layout has been
  replaced.
- The `docname` field in YAML front matter must use the `-latest` suffix
  (e.g. `draft-kerwin-http2-encoded-data-latest`). Actual version numbers
  are derived from git tags, not from the docname.
- Drafts in `Published/` are archived and should not be modified.

## Build system

- This repository uses
  [martinthomson/i-d-template](https://github.com/martinthomson/i-d-template).
  The `lib/` directory is a shallow clone of that repository, auto-cloned on
  first `make`. It is git-ignored and must not be committed.
- Run `make` from the repository root, not from subdirectories.
- Run `make lint` to check for errors; `make fix-lint` to auto-fix.
- Several files are auto-generated by i-d-template and should not be edited
  manually: `README.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `LICENSE.md`, `.editorconfig`,
  `.github/CODEOWNERS`, and all files under `.github/workflows/`. These are
  regenerated by the `update.yml` workflow.

## CI and gh-pages

- The `ghpages.yml` workflow builds all drafts on push to `main` and deploys
  to the `gh-pages` branch. Do not manually edit gh-pages content that is
  generated by this workflow; it will be overwritten.
- The `gh-pages` branch also contains historical content in subdirectories
  (e.g. `file-scheme/`) that predates the i-d-template migration. This
  content is preserved by the workflow and should not be removed.

## Conventions

- Drafts must not contain trailing whitespace. The CI lint check enforces
  this.
- Empty YAML fields should have no trailing space after the colon (e.g.
  `workgroup:` not `workgroup: `).
